payload.name: restore
payload.description: Roll a capability back to an earlier compiled version. Args: <name> [seq]. Emits a data-only restore.requested; the kernel reinstalls its own earlier receipt.

params.name: string — the command or projection to roll back
params.seq: int — optional script.compiled seq from history; 0 = previous version

payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# restore — ask the kernel to roll a capability back to an earlier version.
# Emits a data-only restore.requested {name, seq}; the kernel resolves the
# receipt and reinstalls (only the kernel installs code). seq 0 = previous
# version. Accepts "<name> <seq>" whether passed as one arg or two.
import sys, json
parts = " ".join(sys.argv[1:]).split()
if not parts:
    sys.stderr.write("usage: restore <name> [seq]\n"); sys.exit(1)
name = parts[0]
seq = int(parts[1]) if len(parts) > 1 and parts[1].lstrip("-").isdigit() else 0
print(json.dumps({"name": "restore.requested", "payload": {"name": name, "seq": seq}}))
